 {"id":"bd-904","title":"Implement detect_collisions()","description":"**Warn about path collisions**

## What
Function to detect when different repos would resolve to the same local path.

## Why
With flat layout, org1/utils and org2/utils collide. Users need to know before we overwrite.

## Difference from dedupe
- dedupe: Same repo referenced twice (ok, skip second)
- collision: Different repos mapping to same path (warning!)

## Implementation
```bash
detect_collisions() {
    local -A path_to_url
    local collisions=0
    
    while IFS= read -r spec; do
        local url branch local_name
        parse_repo_spec "$spec" url branch local_name
        
        local path
        path=$(url_to_local_path "$url" "$PROJECTS_DIR" "$LAYOUT")
        
        local norm_url
        norm_url=$(normalize_url "$url")
        
        if [[ -n "${path_to_url[$path]:-}" ]]; then
            local existing="${path_to_url[$path]}"
            if [[ "$existing" != "$norm_url" ]]; then
                log_warn "Collision detected: $path"
                log_warn "  URL 1: $existing"
                log_warn "  URL 2: $norm_url"
                ((collisions++))
            fi
        else
            path_to_url[$path]="$norm_url"
        fi
    done
    
    return $collisions
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Detects different repos at same path
- Doesn't flag same repo twice
- Returns collision count","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:33:03.266022614-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:33:03.266022614-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all repo list management functions: parse_repo_spec (namerefs), load_repo_list, dedupe_repos, detect_collisions, get_all_repos. All tests pass.","labels":["lists","safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-904","depends_on_id":"bd-703","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.391710099-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"bd-904","depends_on_id":"bd-901","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.422048533-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"bd-905","title":"Implement get_all_repos()","description":"**Load and merge all repository lists**

## What
Function to load all list files, merge, dedupe, and check for collisions.

## Why
This is the main entry point for getting the list of repos to process.

## Implementation
```bash
get_all_repos() {
    local repos_dir="$RU_CONFIG_DIR/repos.d"
    
    if [[ ! -d "$repos_dir" ]]; then
        log_warn "No repos.d directory. Run 'ru init' first."
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Collect all repos from all .txt files
    local all_repos
    all_repos=$(
        for f in "$repos_dir"/*.txt; do
            [[ -f "$f" ]] || continue
            load_repo_list "$f"
        done
    )
    
    if [[ -z "$all_repos" ]]; then
        log_warn "No repositories configured. Run 'ru add <repo>' first."
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Check for collisions (warning only)
    echo "$all_repos" | detect_collisions || true
    
    # Dedupe and output
    echo "$all_repos" | dedupe_repos
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Loads from all .txt files in repos.d
- Warns about collisions
- Dedupes results
- Handles empty gracefully","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:33:03.267327372-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:33:03.267327372-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all repo list management functions: parse_repo_spec (namerefs), load_repo_list, dedupe_repos, detect_collisions, get_all_repos. All tests pass.","labels":["lists"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-905","depends_on_id":"bd-901","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.451307773-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"bd-905","depends_on_id":"bd-903","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.482195622-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"bd-905","depends_on_id":"bd-904","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.513296182-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"bd-905b","title":"Define collision handling policy","description":"**Clear policy for path collision handling**

## What
Define what happens when collision is detected (different repos -> same path).

## Why
Current plan detects collisions but doesn't specify the behavior.

## Policy Options
1. **Warn and skip second repo** (safest, current implicit behavior)
2. **Fail fast** (strict, stops processing)
3. **Prompt user** (interactive only)
4. **Auto-rename with suffix** (risky)

## Chosen Policy: Warn and skip
```
Collision detected:
  Path: /home/user/projects/utils
  Configured: org1/utils (will be synced)
  Skipped:    org2/utils (same path)

To fix: Change layout to 'owner-repo' in config:
  ru config --set LAYOUT=owner-repo
```

## Implementation
- First occurrence wins
- Log warning with resolution hint
- Include in summary as 'skipped:collision'
- Exit code 2 (conflicts) if any collisions

## Acceptance Criteria
- Clear messaging about collision
- First-wins policy
- Resolution hint in output
- Documented in README","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:30:11.250738889-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:30:11.250738889-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented in ru script SECTION 9B","labels":["lists","safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-905b","depends_on_id":"bd-9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.858988726-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"bd-905b","depends_on_id":"bd-904","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.890572977-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"bd-9j92","title":"Implement main dashboard view (ntm mode)","description":"Task: Implement Main Dashboard View

Purpose
-------
Full-screen TUI dashboard showing all pending questions, active sessions,
and summary statistics in a organized layout.

Layout Design
-------------
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  ru review                                    Progress: 5/8  Runtime  |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                       |
|  PENDING QUESTIONS (3)                                                |
|  -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
|  [1] ● project-alpha     Issue #42     Priority: CRITICAL             |
|      Context: Authentication failing on Windows                       |
|      > a) Quick fix  b) Full refactor  c) Skip                       |
|                                                                       |
|  [2] ○ project-beta      PR #15        Priority: NORMAL              |
|      [Press Enter to expand]                                          |
|                                                                       |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  ACTIVE SESSIONS                                                      |
|  -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
|  Repo              State         Progress      Health                 |
|  project-delta     GENERATING    80%           Healthy                |
|  project-epsilon   THINKING      60%           Healthy                |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  SUMMARY                                                              |
|  Completed: 4 | Issues: 7 | PRs: 1 | Commits: 12                     |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| [1-9] Answer [Enter] Expand [d] Drill [s] Skip [a] Apply [q] Quit    |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+

Implementation
--------------

Dashboard State
  declare -A DASHBOARD_STATE=(
    [selected_index]=0
    [expanded_question]=""
    [scroll_offset]=0
    [panel_focus]="questions"
  )

render_dashboard()
  clear
  render_header
  render_questions_panel
  render_sessions_panel
  render_summary_panel
  render_footer

render_questions_panel()
  Load questions from queue
  For each question:
    if expanded: show full context
    else: show one-line summary
  Highlight selected with ANSI

render_sessions_panel()
  Query session states
  Format as table with columns

render_summary_panel()
  Load metrics from state
  Format as single line stats

Dashboard Loop
--------------
run_dashboard()
  while true:
    render_dashboard
    read -rsn1 key
    handle_keypress "$key"
    
    # Check for new events
    poll_events

handle_keypress()
  case key:
    1-9: select_and_answer
    Enter: toggle_expand
    d: open_drilldown
    s: skip_current
    q: confirm_quit

Refresh Logic
-------------
- Full refresh every 5 seconds
- Immediate refresh on user action
- Poll for new questions/events

Terminal Handling
-----------------
- Save cursor position
- Use alternate screen buffer
- Restore on exit
- Handle resize (SIGWINCH)

Testing
-------
- Verify layout renders correctly
- Verify keyboard navigation
- Verify refresh updates data
- Verify resize handling
- Test with different terminal sizes

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Dashboard renders cleanly
- [ ] Questions panel shows all pending
- [ ] Sessions panel updates in real-time
- [ ] Keyboard shortcuts work
- [ ] Handles terminal resize","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:40:43.609323436-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T17:59:43.881389549-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T17:59:43.881389549-05:00","close_reason":"Dashboard view fully implemented with questions panel, sessions panel, summary panel, keyboard navigation, and terminal handling","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9j92","depends_on_id":"bd-4ps0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T16:08:34.753044804-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-9s7y","title":"Implement local driver (tmux + stream-json)","description":"# Task: Implement Local Driver (tmux + stream-json)

## Purpose
Implement the session driver interface using tmux and Claude's stream-json output mode. This is the fallback driver when ntm is not available.

## Background
Claude Code supports --output-format stream-json which emits NDJSON events:
- System init with session_id and available tools
- Assistant messages with text and tool_use
- Tool results
- Final result with status

We capture this output and parse it for question detection.

## Implementation

### local_driver_start_session()
```bash
local_driver_start_session() {
    local wt_path="$1"
    local session_name="$2"
    local prompt="$3"
    
    local log_file="$wt_path/.ru/session.log"
    local event_pipe="$wt_path/.ru/events.pipe"
    
    # Create named pipe for event streaming
    rm -f "$event_pipe"
    mkfifo "$event_pipe"
    
    # Build claude command
    local claude_cmd="claude -p $(printf '%q' "$prompt") --output-format stream-json"
    
    # Create tmux session
    tmux new-session -d -s "$session_name" -c "$wt_path" \
        "exec $claude_cmd 2>&1 | tee '$log_file' > '$event_pipe'"
    
    # Return session info
    cat << EOF
{
  "session_id": "$session_name",
  "worktree": "$wt_path",
  "log_file": "$log_file",
  "event_pipe": "$event_pipe",
  "started_at": "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
}
EOF
}
```

### local_driver_send_to_session()
```bash
local_driver_send_to_session() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local message="$2"
    
    # Send via tmux
    tmux send-keys -t "$session_id" "$message" Enter
    
    return $?
}
```

### local_driver_get_session_state()
```bash
local_driver_get_session_state() {
    local session_id="$1"
    
    # Check if tmux session exists
    if ! tmux has-session -t "$session_id" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo '{"state": "complete", "reason": "session_ended"}'
        return
    fi
    
    # Capture last lines of output
    local output
    output=$(tmux capture-pane -t "$session_id" -p -S -50 2>/dev/null)
    
    # Detect state from output
    local state="generating"
    local wait_reason=""
    
    # Check for prompt (waiting)
    if echo "$output" | grep -qE '^\s*>\s*$|Press Enter|Select.*:|Enter.*:'; then
        state="waiting"
        wait_reason="prompt_detected"
    fi
    
    # Check for errors
    if echo "$output" | grep -qiE 'error:|panic:|rate.limit|429'; then
        state="error"
    fi
    
    cat << EOF
{
  "state": "$state",
  "wait_reason": "$wait_reason",
  "session_id": "$session_id"
}
EOF
}
```

### local_driver_stream_events()
```bash
local_driver_stream_events() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local callback="$2"
    local wt_path="$3"
    
    local event_pipe="$wt_path/.ru/events.pipe"
    local log_file="$wt_path/.ru/session.log"
    
    # Parse stream-json events
    while IFS= read -r line; do
        [[ -z "$line" ]] && continue
        
        # Parse JSON event type
        local event_type
        event_type=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.type // empty' 2>/dev/null)
        
        case "$event_type" in
            system)
                local session_id_from_event
                session_id_from_event=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.session_id // empty')
                $callback "init" "$session_id_from_event"
                ;;
            assistant)
                # Check for AskUserQuestion tool_use
                local tool_name
                tool_name=$(echo "$line" | jq -r \
                    '.message.content[]? | select(.type=="tool_use") | .name // empty' 2>/dev/null)
                
                if [[ "$tool_name" == "AskUserQuestion" ]]; then
                    local question_data
                    question_data=$(echo "$line" | jq -c \
                        '.message.content[] | select(.name=="AskUserQuestion") | .input')
                    $callback "question" "$question_data"
                fi
                ;;
            result)
                local status
                status=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.status // "unknown"')
                $callback "complete" "$status"
                break
                ;;
        esac
    done < "$event_pipe"
}
```

### local_driver_stop_session()
```bash
local_driver_stop_session() {
    local session_id="$1"
    
    # Kill tmux session
    tmux kill-session -t "$session_id" 2>/dev/null || true
    
    return 0
}
```

### local_driver_interrupt_session()
```bash
local_driver_interrupt_session() {
    local session_id="$1"
    
    # Send Ctrl+C
    tmux send-keys -t "$session_id" C-c
    
    return 0
}
```

## Session Monitoring Loop
```bash
local_driver_monitor_sessions() {
    local -a sessions=("$@")
    
    while [[ ${#sessions[@]} -gt 0 ]]; do
        local -a active_sessions=()
        
        for session_id in "${sessions[@]}"; do
            local state
            state=$(local_driver_get_session_state "$session_id" | jq -r '.state')
            
            case "$state" in
                complete)
                    handle_session_complete "$session_id"
                    ;;
                error)
                    handle_session_error "$session_id"
                    ;;
                waiting)
                    handle_session_waiting "$session_id"
                    active_sessions+=("$session_id")
                    ;;
                *)
                    active_sessions+=("$session_id")
                    ;;
            esac
        done
        
        sessions=("${active_sessions[@]}")
        sleep 2
    done
}
```

## Testing
- Launch session, verify tmux created
- Send message, verify delivered
- Detect AskUserQuestion event
- Detect session completion
- Interrupt and stop work correctly

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Sessions launch in tmux with stream-json
- [ ] Events parsed from NDJSON stream
- [ ] AskUserQuestion detected
- [ ] Messages sent via tmux send-keys
- [ ] Session cleanup works
- [ ] Works without ntm installed","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:35:14.53945729-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:42:46.375298473-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T16:42:46.375298473-05:00","close_reason":"Local driver implemented with: local_driver_start_session (tmux + stream-json), local_driver_send_to_session, local_driver_get_session_state, local_driver_stop_session, local_driver_interrupt_session, local_driver_stream_events (stub for bd-8zt6), local_driver_list_sessions, local_driver_session_alive. Driver routing via _enable_local_driver(). ShellCheck passes.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9s7y","depends_on_id":"bd-jm89","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:44:54.79309431-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-gptu","title":"Implement rate-limit governor (adaptive concurrency)","description":"Task: Implement Rate-Limit Governor

Purpose
-------
Dynamically adjust parallelism based on real rate limit data from GitHub
API and model responses, preventing throttling and cascading failures.

Background: Why Adaptive?
-------------------------
Static parallelism is dangerous:
- Too high: hit rate limits, get blocked
- Too low: waste time when limits are fine
- Limits vary by time, account tier, prior usage

Solution: query real limits, adjust dynamically.

Governor State
--------------
GOVERNOR_STATE associative array:
- github_remaining: remaining API calls
- github_reset: when limit resets (epoch)
- model_in_backoff: currently backing off?
- model_backoff_until: when backoff ends
- effective_parallelism: current allowed sessions
- circuit_breaker_open: emergency stop?

Implementation
--------------

start_rate_limit_governor()
  Run in background during review:
  while review lock held:
    update_github_rate_limit
    check_model_rate_limit
    adjust_parallelism
    sleep 30

update_github_rate_limit()
  Query: gh api rate_limit
  Extract: .resources.core.remaining and .reset
  If remaining < 500: log warning, reduce parallelism

check_model_rate_limit()
  Scan session logs for 429/rate limit patterns
  If multiple recent hits:
    Set model_in_backoff = true
    Set backoff_until = now + 60 seconds

adjust_parallelism()
  Start with target = REVIEW_PARALLEL (default 4)
  
  If github_remaining < 1000: target /= 2
  If model_in_backoff: target = 1
  
  Count recent errors in sliding window
  If errors > 5 in 5 minutes:
    Open circuit breaker
    target = 0
    Log emergency pause
  
  Set effective_parallelism = target

can_start_new_session()
  Return false if:
  - circuit_breaker_open
  - model_in_backoff
  - active_sessions >= effective_parallelism
  Otherwise true

Circuit Breaker
---------------
Pattern for handling cascading failures:
- CLOSED: normal operation
- OPEN: too many failures, pause all
- HALF-OPEN: after cooldown, try one (not implemented yet)

Metrics Tracked
---------------
- API calls per minute
- 429 responses per session
- Session error rate
- Effective vs requested parallelism

Integration Points
------------------
- Called before starting new session
- Called in session monitor loop
- Updates TUI status display
- Writes metrics for analytics

Testing
-------
- Mock gh api rate_limit responses
- Simulate 429 responses
- Verify parallelism adjusts
- Verify circuit breaker triggers

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Queries real GitHub rate limits
- [ ] Detects model rate limits from logs
- [ ] Adjusts parallelism dynamically
- [ ] Circuit breaker prevents cascading failures
- [ ] Metrics tracked for analysis","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:38:47.005795803-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:38:47.005795803-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-gptu","depends_on_id":"bd-jm89","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T16:17:00.313011296-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-hmw8","title":"Implement command validation and blocking","description":"Security: Validate commands before execution in agent sessions.

Three categories:

SAFE_BASH_COMMANDS (always allowed):
  git, grep, find, ls, cat, make, npm, cargo, go, python, pytest,
  shellcheck, eslint, prettier, jq, sed, awk, sort, diff

APPROVAL_REQUIRED_COMMANDS (human confirmation):
  rm, mv, cp (file operations)
  curl, wget (network)
  docker, kubectl (containers)

BLOCKED_COMMANDS (never allowed):
  sudo, su, chmod +x, chown, eval, exec

Special gh handling:
  READ allowed: gh issue view, gh pr view, gh issue list
  BLOCKED in Plan mode: gh issue comment, gh issue close, gh pr merge

validate_agent_command() checks command against lists, returns:
  0 = allowed
  1 = blocked
  2 = needs approval

Called by pre-exec hook in agent sessions.

Acceptance: Blocked commands rejected, approval commands queued, gh mutations blocked in Plan mode.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:43:25.152161697-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T17:02:09.080859708-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T17:02:09.080859708-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented command validation with 19 passing tests","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hmw8","depends_on_id":"bd-9s7y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:17.523931567-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-jen3","title":"Implement review command exit codes and error classification","description":"# Task: Implement Review Command Exit Codes

## Purpose
Define and implement consistent exit codes for `ru review` following the ru exit code conventions, enabling automation and scripting.

## Background: ru Exit Code Convention
From AGENTS.md, ru uses these exit codes:
- 0 = Success (all repos synced/reviewed)
- 1 = Partial failure (some repos failed)
- 2 = Conflicts exist (needs resolution)
- 3 = Dependency/system error (gh missing, auth failed)
- 4 = Invalid arguments
- 5 = Interrupted (use --resume)

## Review-Specific Exit Codes

### Exit 0: Success
- All sessions completed successfully
- All questions answered
- All approved changes applied (if --apply)

### Exit 1: Partial Failure
- Some sessions failed but others succeeded
- Some repos could not be reviewed (network, rate limit)
- Partial progress was made

### Exit 2: Conflicts/Review Needed
- Agent detected merge conflicts in worktree
- Quality gates failed (tests/lint)
- Changes require human intervention before apply

### Exit 3: Dependency/System Error
- Claude Code not installed or not in PATH
- gh CLI not authenticated
- tmux not available (for local driver)
- ntm not available (when --mode=ntm specified)

### Exit 4: Invalid Arguments
- Unknown flags
- Invalid --mode value
- Invalid --priority value
- Conflicting options (--plan with --push)

### Exit 5: Interrupted
- SIGINT/SIGTERM received
- --max-runtime exceeded
- --max-questions exceeded
- Checkpoint saved, use --resume to continue

## Implementation

### Error Classification Function
```bash
classify_review_error() {
    local error_type="$1"
    local context="$2"
    
    case "$error_type" in
        session_failed|rate_limited|network_error)
            echo "partial"  # Exit 1
            ;;
        merge_conflict|quality_gate_failed|tests_failed)
            echo "conflict"  # Exit 2
            ;;
        missing_dependency|auth_failed|no_driver)
            echo "system"  # Exit 3
            ;;
        invalid_flag|bad_mode|conflicting_options)
            echo "invalid"  # Exit 4
            ;;
        interrupted|timeout|max_questions)
            echo "interrupted"  # Exit 5
            ;;
        *)
            echo "unknown"
            ;;
    esac
}
```

### Exit Code Aggregation
```bash
aggregate_exit_code() {
    local -a exit_codes=("$@")
    local max_code=0
    
    for code in "${exit_codes[@]}"; do
        [[ $code -gt $max_code ]] && max_code=$code
    done
    
    # Special case: 5 (interrupted) takes precedence over 1 (partial)
    if [[ " ${exit_codes[*]} " =~ " 5 " ]]; then
        echo 5
        return
    fi
    
    echo $max_code
}
```

### Final Exit Handling
```bash
finalize_review_exit() {
    local exit_code="$1"
    
    case "$exit_code" in
        0) log_success "Review completed successfully" ;;
        1) log_warn "Review completed with partial failures" ;;
        2) log_error "Review blocked by conflicts - manual resolution needed" ;;
        3) log_error "Review failed due to system/dependency error" ;;
        4) log_error "Invalid arguments" ;;
        5) log_warn "Review interrupted - use --resume to continue" ;;
    esac
    
    exit "$exit_code"
}
```

## Testing
- Verify each exit code for its conditions
- Verify --resume works after exit 5
- Verify aggregation chooses worst code
- Verify CI can parse exit codes

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 6 exit codes defined and documented
- [ ] Error classification works correctly
- [ ] Exit code aggregation from multiple sessions
- [ ] Clear log message for each exit code
- [ ] --resume only available after exit 5

+{"id":"bd-jen3","title":"Implement review command exit codes and error classification","description":"# Task: Implement Review Command Exit Codes

## Purpose
Define and implement consistent exit codes for `ru review` following the ru exit code conventions, enabling automation and scripting.

## Background: ru Exit Code Convention
From AGENTS.md, ru uses these exit codes:
- 0 = Success (all repos synced/reviewed)
- 1 = Partial failure (some repos failed)
- 2 = Conflicts exist (needs resolution)
- 3 = Dependency/system error (gh missing, auth failed)
- 4 = Invalid arguments
- 5 = Interrupted (use --resume)

## Review-Specific Exit Codes

### Exit 0: Success
- All sessions completed successfully
- All questions answered
- All approved changes applied (if --apply)

### Exit 1: Partial Failure
- Some sessions failed but others succeeded
- Some repos could not be reviewed (network, rate limit)
- Partial progress was made

### Exit 2: Conflicts/Review Needed
- Agent detected merge conflicts in worktree
- Quality gates failed (tests/lint)
- Changes require human intervention before apply

### Exit 3: Dependency/System Error
- Claude Code not installed or not in PATH
- gh CLI not authenticated
- tmux not available (for local driver)
- ntm not available (when --mode=ntm specified)

### Exit 4: Invalid Arguments
- Unknown flags
- Invalid --mode value
- Invalid --priority value
- Conflicting options (--plan with --push)

### Exit 5: Interrupted
- SIGINT/SIGTERM received
- --max-runtime exceeded
- --max-questions exceeded
- Checkpoint saved, use --resume to continue

## Implementation

### Error Classification Function
```bash
classify_review_error() {
    local error_type="$1"
    local context="$2"
    
    case "$error_type" in
        session_failed|rate_limited|network_error)
            echo "partial"  # Exit 1
            ;;
        merge_conflict|quality_gate_failed|tests_failed)
            echo "conflict"  # Exit 2
            ;;
        missing_dependency|auth_failed|no_driver)
            echo "system"  # Exit 3
            ;;
        invalid_flag|bad_mode|conflicting_options)
            echo "invalid"  # Exit 4
            ;;
        interrupted|timeout|max_questions)
            echo "interrupted"  # Exit 5
            ;;
        *)
            echo "unknown"
            ;;
    esac
}
```

### Exit Code Aggregation
```bash
aggregate_exit_code() {
    local -a exit_codes=("$@")
    local max_code=0
    
    for code in "${exit_codes[@]}"; do
        [[ $code -gt $max_code ]] && max_code=$code
    done
    
    # Special case: 5 (interrupted) takes precedence over 1 (partial)
    if [[ " ${exit_codes[*]} " =~ " 5 " ]]; then
        echo 5
        return
    fi
    
    echo $max_code
}
```

### Final Exit Handling
```bash
finalize_review_exit() {
    local exit_code="$1"
    
    case "$exit_code" in
        0) log_success "Review completed successfully" ;;
        1) log_warn "Review completed with partial failures" ;;
        2) log_error "Review blocked by conflicts - manual resolution needed" ;;
        3) log_error "Review failed due to system/dependency error" ;;
        4) log_error "Invalid arguments" ;;
        5) log_warn "Review interrupted - use --resume to continue" ;;
    esac
    
    exit "$exit_code"
}
```

## Testing
- Verify each exit code for its conditions
- Verify --resume works after exit 5
- Verify aggregation chooses worst code
- Verify CI can parse exit codes

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 6 exit codes defined and documented
- [ ] Error classification works correctly
- [ ] Exit code aggregation from multiple sessions
- [ ] Clear log message for each exit code
- [ ] --resume only available after exit 5

 {"id":"bd-jm89","title":"Define unified session driver interface","description":"# Task: Define Unified Session Driver Interface

## Purpose
Create an abstract interface that both ntm and local drivers implement, enabling graceful degradation and consistent behavior regardless of which driver is used.

## Background: Why a Unified Interface?
- ntm provides advanced orchestration but may not be installed
- Local driver (tmux + stream-json) works everywhere
- Same code should work with either driver
- Enables testing without full ntm dependency
- Future drivers (remote, cloud) can plug in

## Interface Definition

### Driver Capabilities
```bash
# Query what driver can do
driver_capabilities() {
    # Returns JSON with capabilities
    cat << EOF
{
  "name": "local",
  "parallel_sessions": true,
  "activity_detection": false,
  "health_monitoring": false,
  "question_routing": true
}
EOF
}
```

### Core Operations
```bash
# Start a new Claude session
# Args: worktree_path, session_name, initial_prompt
# Returns: session_id
driver_start_session() {
    local wt_path="$1"
    local session_name="$2"
    local prompt="$3"
    # Implementation varies by driver
}

# Send message/answer to session
# Args: session_id, message
# Returns: 0 on success
driver_send_to_session() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local message="$2"
}

# Get current session state
# Args: session_id
# Returns: JSON with state info
driver_get_session_state() {
    local session_id="$1"
    # Returns: {"state": "generating|waiting|complete|error", ...}
}

# Stop/kill a session
# Args: session_id
driver_stop_session() {
    local session_id="$1"
}

# Interrupt session (Ctrl+C equivalent)
# Args: session_id
driver_interrupt_session() {
    local session_id="$1"
}
```

### Event Streaming
```bash
# Stream events from session
# Args: session_id, callback_function
# Callback receives: event_type, event_data
driver_stream_events() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local callback="$2"
    # Calls: $callback "question" "$question_json"
    # Calls: $callback "complete" "$status"
    # Calls: $callback "error" "$error_msg"
}
```

### Normalized Event Schema
Both drivers emit the same event format:
```json
{
  "type": "init|generating|waiting|complete|error",
  "session_id": "...",
  "timestamp": "2025-01-04T10:30:00Z",
  "wait_info": {
    "reason": "ask_user_question|agent_question_text|external_prompt|unknown",
    "context": "...",
    "options": ["a) ...", "b) ..."],
    "recommended": "a",
    "risk_level": "low|medium|high"
  }
}
```

## Driver Selection
```bash
detect_review_driver() {
    # Try ntm first (preferred)
    if command -v ntm &>/dev/null; then
        if ntm --robot-status &>/dev/null 2>&1; then
            log_verbose "Using ntm driver (robot mode available)"
            echo "ntm"
            return
        fi
        log_verbose "ntm found but robot mode unavailable"
    fi

    # Fall back to local driver
    if command -v tmux &>/dev/null && command -v claude &>/dev/null; then
        log_verbose "Using local driver (tmux + stream-json)"
        echo "local"
        return
    fi

    log_error "No suitable driver available"
    echo "unsupported"
}
```

## Driver Loading
```bash
load_review_driver() {
    local driver="$1"
    
    case "$driver" in
        ntm)
            source "$RU_LIB_DIR/drivers/ntm.sh"
            ;;
        local)
            source "$RU_LIB_DIR/drivers/local.sh"
            ;;
        *)
            log_error "Unknown driver: $driver"
            return 1
            ;;
    esac
    
    REVIEW_DRIVER="$driver"
}
```

## Implementation Notes
- Functions prefixed with driver_ are implemented per-driver
- Common code calls driver_* functions
- Drivers can extend interface with driver-specific features
- Capabilities check enables graceful feature degradation

## Testing
- Mock driver for unit tests
- Verify interface consistency between drivers
- Test driver selection logic
- Verify event normalization

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Interface defined with all required operations
- [ ] Driver selection works correctly
- [ ] Event schema documented and consistent
- [ ] Capabilities query implemented
- [ ] Driver loading mechanism works","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:35:14.284949263-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:39:51.577905888-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T16:39:51.577905888-05:00","close_reason":"Unified driver interface implemented with: load_review_driver(), driver_capabilities(), driver_start_session(), driver_send_to_session(), driver_get_session_state(), driver_stop_session(), driver_interrupt_session(), driver_stream_events(), driver_list_sessions(), driver_session_alive(). Includes event schema documentation.  {"id":"bd-k1kx","title":"Implement ntm driver using robot mode API","description":"Task: Implement ntm Driver Using Robot Mode API

Purpose
-------
Implement the unified session driver interface using ntm robot mode,
providing advanced orchestration capabilities beyond the local driver.

ntm Robot Mode API
------------------
ntm exposes a JSON API via command-line flags:

1. --robot-spawn SESSION_NAME
   Create new tmux session with Claude panes
   Returns: {"success": true, "session": "...", "panes": [...]}

2. --robot-status
   Query all session states
   Returns: {"sessions": {...}, "questions": [...], "system": {...}}

3. --robot-health=SESSION
   Get health metrics for session
   Returns: {"agents": {...}, "alerts": [...]}

4. --robot-activity=PANE_ID
   Get activity state for specific pane
   Returns: {"state": "GENERATING", "velocity": 12.5, "confidence": 0.85}

5. --robot-route=SESSION
   Get recommended pane for new work
   Returns: {"recommendation": {"pane_id": "...", "reason": "..."}}

6. --robot-send --pane=PANE_ID --msg=MESSAGE
   Send message to pane
   Returns: {"status": "ok", "delivered": true}

Implementation
--------------

ntm_driver_start_session()
  - Call ntm --robot-spawn with --cc=$parallel for Claude panes
  - Record pane assignments in state
  - Return session info JSON

ntm_driver_get_session_state()
  - Call ntm --robot-activity for pane
  - Map ntm states to unified states
  - Include wait_info if WAITING

ntm_driver_send_to_session()
  - Call ntm --robot-send with pane and message
  - Verify delivery confirmation

ntm_driver_stream_events()
  - Poll --robot-activity periodically
  - Detect state transitions
  - Call callback on significant events

ntm_driver_stop_session()
  - Use tmux kill-session (ntm manages cleanup)

State Mapping
-------------
ntm state      -> Unified state
GENERATING     -> generating
WAITING        -> waiting (with wait_info)
THINKING       -> thinking
STALLED        -> stalled
ERROR          -> error

Health Monitoring Integration
-----------------------------
Start background health monitor:
  while session active:
    health = ntm --robot-health
    for alert in health.alerts:
      handle_alert(alert)
    sleep 10

Advantages Over Local Driver
----------------------------
- Velocity-based detection (more accurate)
- Health monitoring built-in
- Routing for optimal pane selection
- Better stall detection
- Structured error patterns

Testing
-------
- Mock ntm commands for unit tests
- Integration test with real ntm
- Verify state mapping correct
- Verify health alerts handled

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] All driver interface methods implemented
- [ ] State mapping matches unified schema
- [ ] Health monitoring integrated
- [ ] Routing recommendation used
- [ ] Graceful fallback if ntm unavailable","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:38:45.086444687-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:52:26.596085263-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T16:52:26.596085263-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented full ntm driver with all interface methods: start_session, stop_session, send_to_session, get_session_state, stream_events, list_sessions, session_alive, interrupt_session. State mapping matches unified schema. Health monitoring integrated with velocity-based detection.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-k1kx","depends_on_id":"bd-jm89","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:11.685975198-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
